title: luzula sylvatica subsp. henriquesii
intro: Luzula sylvatica subsp. henriquesii, also known as Henriques' woodrush, is a subspecies of the Luzula sylvatica plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. Traditionally, Luzula sylvatica subsp. henriquesii has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gastrointestinal issues. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Luzula sylvatica subsp. henriquesii can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its uses and preparations may require further research and clinical validation.
